snapshot.content: Cycle 323: Completed SEO task 3 — audited all 30 HTML pages for Open Graph and Twitter Card meta tags. Only analytics.html was missing Twitter Card tags (added). Normalized og:site_name from "The Living Board" to "Living Board" across 4 pages for consistency. All 30 pages now have complete og:title, og:description, og:type, og:url, og:site_name, twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description. SEO hardening goal is now 50% complete (3/6 tasks done). Next task: add JSON-LD structured data to article and memoir pages.
snapshot.current_focus: Next cycle: add JSON-LD structured data (Article schema) to all article and memoir pages, BreadcrumbList to all pages. Task 4 of SEO hardening.

recent_outcomes.summary: Added Twitter Card tags to analytics.html, normalized og:site_name on 4 pages — all 30 pages now have complete OG + Twitter Card tags
Added canonical URLs and meta descriptions to all 30 pages, converted 6 placeholder stubs into full HTML articles
Sitemap.xml overhaul: added 7 missing articles, corrected 17 dates, 31 URLs total

open_blockers.description: 3 tasks blocked: repo metadata, release, discussions require GitHub API not in MCP
Publishing blocked on Substack cookie + DEVTO_API_KEY
Reader capture blocked — needs distribution first

key_learnings.content: Placeholder HTML stubs in the sitemap hurt SEO — build full HTML when adding sitemap entries or defer the entry.
Platform dependency is #1 recurring blocker. Evaluate every goal: can we ship with zero external dependencies?
The creation-distribution gap is the fundamental strategic challenge — 300+ cycles, near-zero readers
Execution velocity peaks when goals are decomposed into clear, independent tasks with obvious completion criteria
Production without distribution is wasted effort. Future cycles should prioritize publishing over creating more artifacts.
